Root Cause: The function isPrototypePropertyAssign is not correctly handling the case where the property assignment is chained.

Suggestion 1: Modify the isPrototypePropertyAssign function to correctly handle chained property assignments
The function isPrototypePropertyAssign needs to be modified to correctly handle the case where the property assignment is chained. This can be done by checking if the child node of the GETPROP node is also a GETPROP node, and if so, recursively checking its child nodes.

Suggestion 2: Add a recursive function to handle chained property assignments
A recursive function can be added to handle chained property assignments. This function can be called from the isPrototypePropertyAssign function to check if the property assignment is chained.

Suggestion 3: Use a loop to handle chained property assignments
A loop can be used to handle chained property assignments. The loop can iterate over the child nodes of the GETPROP node until it finds a node that is not a GETPROP node, and then check if the property assignment is a prototype property assignment.
